prose:
Frontier-orbital analysis of TEMPO (2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine-1-oxyl) was performed using DFT at the tier 'standard' level — B3LYP/def2-TZVP — in PySCF via the chemkit frontier-orbitals skill. The molecule is an open-shell doublet radical (charge 0, multiplicity 2), so UKS was used. The geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied .xyz file (single-point; not re-optimised at this level). The calculation was run in the gas phase with no implicit solvent. Density fitting (RI) was OFF — exact four-centre integrals were used. The SCF converged in 12 cycles. No warnings were reported. The integrity check returned trustworthy = true, status = ok.

Key results:
  HOMO (SOMO, index 86): −6.3375439 eV
  LUMO (index 87):        −2.4651235 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap:           3.8724203 eV  ← headline value

Additional Koopmans'-theorem-derived quantities:
  Vertical IP:             6.3375439 eV
  Vertical EA:             2.4651235 eV
  Electronegativity:       4.4013337 eV
  Chemical hardness (η):   1.9362102 eV
  Chemical softness (σ):   0.51647286 eV⁻¹
  Electrophilicity (ω):    5.0024886 eV

Total electronic energy: −483.88366 Hartree (−13167.145 eV). This energy zero is method- and basis-specific and is not comparable across methods.

Command: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 2
Output JSON: tempo_frontier_dft.json
